title: PBMC Stimulation with Peptide Pools and Fluorospot Assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2clag","text":"The purpose of this protocol is to stimulate PBMCs with peptide pools for 14 days followed by Fluorospot assay.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Add 10 mL, media (complete RPMI with 5% human serum) and 20 µL, Benzonase per vial of cells to a 50 ml tube.
Place PBMC vials in 37 °C water bath for approximately 60-90s. 00:01:30
Before cells are completely thawed, add them to the prepared 50 ml tube.
Pour off media and resuspend in media.
Count cells and record viability (trypan blue).
Pour off media and resuspend according to cell count to a final concentration of 4x10 6 cells/ml.
Add 0.5 mL, cells per well and 0.5 mL, stimulus (5µg/ml final concentration) . Plate Layout
Add media supplemented with 10 U/mL (final concentration) of rIL-2 every 3-4 days.
Day 4 – add 1 mL, media supplemented with IL-2.
Day 7 and 11 – replace 1 mL, media supplemented with IL-2 without disturbing cells.
Record peptide pool information: Donor Location Vials Cells/ml (x10^6 ) Volume (mL) Total # PBMCs (x10^6 ) Volume to reach 4x10^6 /ml (mL) % viability
